visual_elements.description: Evaporation occurs when the sun heats bodies of water, causing surface water to vaporize.
The evaporated water rises into the atmosphere where it cools and condenses forming clouds in a process called condensation.
When these water droplets become heavy enough combine with other droplets or ice particles fall as precipitation (rain, snow) to land surfaces.
Once on the ground, some of the water is absorbed by plants and later transpired back into the atmosphere through a process called evapotranspiration.
Some water infiltrates soil becoming part of groundwater or runs off directly into bodies of water like rivers and oceans.
